title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests · Code Recall
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs slugs, permalinks, redirects, canonicals, and route manifests from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.
Define Unicode normalization, transliteration or preservation, case, separators, reserved names, empties, length, and collision behavior.
Select explicit or derived permalinks, trailing-slash convention, date behavior, language prefix, and base path with migration evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.instruction:
review case — A review publishing team handles case 7395 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review publishing team handles case 4073 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
